h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, I think I can see part of it
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the feeling that their home speech is 'sloppy' while trying to identify grammatical patterns.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I thought 'statistically significant' was enough to call it important in the briefing, but that only rules out zero, not 'too small to matter.'
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically build their understanding of how to qualify statistical significance in the briefing.
The learner will continue to practice and refine their presentation of the canyon's geological time scale.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought “presumed innocent” meant the court was saying the accused probably did not do it, like a factual starting point.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I see that is not quite right.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to struggle with the distinction and ask the tutor to resolve their persistent confusion about why 'not guilty' sounds like a factual statement.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, the threshold is not a deer-count line; it is the fenced/open split repeating across zones, lasting through two regeneration windows, and matching signs that the herd is getting stressed.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to ask for rigid, quantitative thresholds to define a trophic cascade.
The learner will continue to tentatively weigh the conflicting evidence of their eyes against the scale's reading.
